children.label: A
children.text: a State, Indian Tribe, municipality, irrigation district, water district, wastewater district, or other organization with water or power delivery authority;

children.label: B
children.text: a State, regional, or local authority, the members of which include 1 or more organizations with water or power delivery authority; or

children.label: C
children.text: an agency established under State law for the joint exercise of powers or a combination of entities described in subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: Eligible project
children.label: 2 Eligible project
children.text: The term “eligible project” means a project described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Program
children.label: 3 Program
children.text: The term “program” means the grant program established under subsection (b).

children.heading: Reclamation State
children.label: 4 Reclamation State
children.text: The term “Reclamation State” means a State or territory described in the first section of the Act of (; , chapter 1093).June 17, 190243 U.S.C. 39132 Stat. 388

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a program to provide grants to eligible entities on a competitive basis for the planning, design, and construction of large-scale water recycling and reuse projects that provide substantial water supply and other benefits to the Reclamation States in accordance with this section.

children.label: A
children.text: municipal, industrial, domestic, or agricultural wastewater; or

children.label: B
children.text: impaired groundwater or surface water;
has a total estimated cost of $500,000,000 or more;
is located in a Reclamation State;
is constructed, operated, and maintained by an eligible entity; and
provides a Federal benefit in accordance with the reclamation laws.

children.label: A
children.text: is technically and financially feasible;

children.label: B
children.text: provides a Federal benefit in accordance with the reclamation laws; and

children.label: C
children.text: is consistent with applicable Federal and State laws;
the eligible entity has sufficient non-Federal funding available to complete the eligible project, as determined by the Secretary;
the eligible entity is financially solvent, as determined by the Secretary; and

not later than 30 days after the date on which the Secretary concurs with the determinations under paragraph (1) with respect to the eligible project, the Secretary submits to Congress written notice of the determinations.

children.label: A
children.text: water supply reliability benefits for drought-stricken States and communities;

children.label: B
children.text: fish and wildlife benefits; and

children.label: C
children.text: water quality improvements.

The eligible project is likely to reduce impacts on environmental resources from water projects owned or operated by Federal and State agencies, including through measurable reductions in water diversions from imperiled ecosystems.
The eligible project would advance water management plans across a multi-State area, such as drought contingency plans in the Colorado River Basin.
The eligible project is regional in nature.
The eligible project is collaboratively developed or supported by multiple stakeholders.

children.heading: Federal cost share
children.label: 1 Federal cost share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of any project provided a grant under the program shall not exceed 25 percent of the total cost of the eligible project.

children.heading: Total dollar cap
children.label: 2 Total dollar cap
children.text: The Secretary shall not impose a total dollar cap on Federal contributions for all eligible individual projects provided a grant under the program.

children.heading: Nonreimbursable funds
children.label: 3 Nonreimbursable funds
children.text: Any funds provided by the Secretary to an eligible entity under the program shall be considered nonreimbursable.

children.label: A
children.text: the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act ( et seq.);43 U.S.C. 390h

children.label: B
children.text: section 4(a) of the Water Desalination Act of 1996 ( note; ) for eligible desalination projects; or42 U.S.C. 10301Public Law 104–298

children.label: C
children.text: section 1602(e) of the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act ().43 U.S.C. 390h(e)

children.heading: Environmental laws
children.label: g Environmental laws
children.text: In providing a grant for an eligible project under the program, the Secretary shall comply with all applicable environmental laws, including the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Guidance
children.label: h Guidance
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall issue guidance on the implementation of the program, including guidelines for the preparation of feasibility studies or equivalent studies by eligible entities.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Annual report
children.label: 1 Annual report
children.text: At the end of each fiscal year, the Secretary shall make available on the website of the Department of the Interior an annual report that lists each eligible project for which a grant has been awarded under this section during the fiscal year.

children.heading: Assessment
children.label: A Assessment
children.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct an assessment of the administrative establishment, solicitation, selection, and justification process with respect to the funding of grants under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the adequacy and effectiveness of the process by which each eligible project was selected, if applicable; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the justification and criteria used for the selection of each eligible project, if applicable.

children.heading: Treatment of conveyance
children.label: j Treatment of conveyance
children.text: The Secretary shall consider the planning, design, and construction of a conveyance system for an eligible project to be eligible for grant funding under the program.

children.heading: Termination of authority
children.label: k Termination of authority
children.text: The authority to carry out this section terminates on the date that is 5 years after .November 15, 2021
